title: Detecting Synthetic Images
concept: Forensics models look for digital artifacts in StyleGAN faces (e.g. asymmetrical earrings, weird hair borders, check pupil centers) to detect fakes.

options.text: It manually photops real people's portraits together.
It generates realistic faces from scratch using neural networks.
It only modifies existing photos to add sunglasses or hats.

exercises.prompt: Yesterday we looked at StyleGAN. How does it create faces?
exercises.explain: StyleGAN uses a special neural network that learns to generate highly realistic, high-resolution human faces from scratch.

cards.body: Have you ever looked at a profile picture online and wondered, "Is this person actually real?" StyleGAN creates faces so photorealistic that our eyes are easily fooled. But look closer: the AI leaves tiny, hidden mistakes!
cards.title: Spot the AI Mistake!

cards.body: AI Forensics Models are specialized computer vision programs trained to detect these synthetic images. They do this by looking for digital artifacts—weird mistakes, logic errors, or structural glitches that humans might miss but math can spot.
cards.title: What is an AI Forensics Model?

cards.body: Imagine a Master Forger trying to copy a famous painting. They get the colors and face shape perfectly right, but they forget that human eyes have round pupils, or they paint two completely different earrings. Forensics models are like art appraisers checking for these sloppy mistakes.
cards.title: The Art Appraiser

cards.body: Look at a StyleGAN-generated portrait. You might notice the left earring is a silver hoop, while the right one is a pearl stud! Or notice the hair blending weirdly into the background like paint melting. These are telltale signs of AI generation.
cards.title: Asymmetrical Accessories

cards.body: Myth: AI faces are perfect because computers don't make mistakes. Fact: AI doesn't understand human anatomy or physics! It doesn't know that earrings usually match, or that pupils should be centered. It only knows patterns, leading to strange glitches.
cards.title: Are AI Faces Perfect?

right.text: Asymmetrical earrings, irregular pupil shapes, and blurry hair-to-background transitions.

left.text: Perfectly round, centered pupils, matching earrings, and clean hair borders.

options.text: By analyzing physical inconsistencies like mismatched earrings and weird pupil shapes.
By searching a database of every real human face in the world.
By checking if the photo has a watermark saying "Created by AI".

exercises.explain: Forensics models look for digital artifacts and physical inconsistencies, like mismatched earrings or warped pupils, to identify synthetic faces.
exercises.prompt: How do forensics models detect StyleGAN-generated faces?
True or False: Asymmetrical earrings are a common digital artifact found in AI-generated portraits.
exercises.explain: True! StyleGAN often struggles with physical symmetry and logic, leading to mismatched accessories like earrings or glasses.

exercises.sentence: To detect synthetic faces, forensic neural networks look for digital [blank] like strange hair borders and misaligned pupils.
exercises.explain: Forensics models look for digital artifacts—which are visual errors or glitches—to tell if a face is generated by an AI.
exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the concept:
Which of these images is most likely generated by StyleGAN?

options.text: A studio portrait with a slightly out-of-focus but clean studio background.
A portrait where the subject's hair strands melt unnaturally into a blurry, distorted background.

exercises.explain: AI generators often struggle with background details and fine hair strands, causing them to blend or melt together unnaturally.

Forensics models search for physical and structural inconsistencies—such as mismatched earrings or misshapen pupils—to catch fake faces. Tomorrow, we will look at how GANs can be used for data augmentation in medical imaging!
exercises.prompt: What is the primary way forensics models catch StyleGAN fakes?

options.text: They search for structural flaws like mismatched earrings, misaligned pupil centers, and unnatural hair borders.
They rely on the image's metadata to read the camera model that took it.
They check if the person in the photo is wearing sunglasses.
